questions.question: Where can you open Notion’s Marketplace from within the app?

options.text: By clicking the ‘Templates’ link in the Notion sidebar
options.explanation: The article states you visit Marketplace by going to ‘Templates’ in the sidebar.

options.text: By selecting ‘Settings & Members’ in the top‑right menu
options.explanation: ‘Settings & Members’ opens account settings, not the Marketplace.

options.text: By typing marketplace.notion.com in your browser
options.explanation: That URL is not the in‑app way to open Marketplace.

options.text: By clicking the ‘Help & Feedback’ button
options.explanation: ‘Help & Feedback’ leads to help articles, not the Marketplace.

questions.question: Which of the following pieces of information is NOT displayed under each template in Marketplace?

options.text: The template’s overall rating
options.explanation: Ratings are listed under each template.

options.text: The number of times the template has been added
options.explanation: The article mentions this count is shown.

options.text: The creator’s personal email address
options.explanation: Creator email is not displayed; you can only contact them via the ‘Contact creator’ option.

options.text: The Notion features included in the template
options.explanation: Feature list is part of the template details.

questions.question: What must you be using in order to add a template to your workspace?

options.text: The Notion mobile app
options.explanation: The note specifies you must be on desktop or web, not mobile.

options.text: A logged‑in session on Notion’s desktop or web app
options.explanation: You must be logged in and on desktop or web to add a template.

options.text: A third‑party browser extension
options.explanation: No extension is required.

options.text: An incognito browser window
options.explanation: Incognito would not retain your login session.

questions.question: If a template you purchase contains a form, what is the recommended step after duplicating it?

options.text: Delete the form block entirely
options.explanation: You don’t need to delete it; you need to recreate it.

options.text: Set up a separate version of the form from scratch
options.explanation: The article advises creating a new form so responses go to you, not the original creator.

options.text: Share the form with the original creator
options.explanation: Sharing isn’t required and could expose your data.

options.text: Leave the form as is; it will automatically redirect responses
options.explanation: Responses will still go to the original creator unless you recreate the form.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about coupons when buying a paid template is true?

options.text: You can apply multiple coupons to a single purchase
options.explanation: The article states you can’t use more than one coupon per purchase.

options.text: Coupons never expire
options.explanation: Coupons may expire, as noted.

options.text: If a coupon expires, you can still use it if you have a backup code
options.explanation: There’s no mention of backup codes; expired coupons are invalid.

options.text: You can enter a coupon code during checkout, but only one per purchase
options.explanation: The checkout process allows a single coupon code per purchase.

questions.question: After successfully purchasing a template, what immediate feedback does Notion provide?

options.text: A pop‑up asking you to rate the template
options.explanation: Rating is optional and not shown immediately after purchase.

options.text: A message confirming the purchase and another confirming the template is ready for use
options.explanation: The article mentions both messages appear after a successful purchase.

options.text: An email with a download link
options.explanation: Templates are added directly to your workspace, not emailed.

options.text: A prompt to share the template on social media
options.explanation: No such prompt is described.

questions.question: Which templates will appear in the “Added” menu of Marketplace?

options.text: All templates you have ever opened, regardless of purchase method
options.explanation: Only those added or purchased directly on Marketplace appear.

options.text: Only templates you added or purchased directly on Marketplace
options.explanation: The note specifies that third‑party purchases are excluded.

options.text: Templates you have liked on the community forum
options.explanation: Liking a template does not add it to the list.

options.text: Templates that are currently free
options.explanation: Free vs paid status isn’t the filter for the Added menu.

questions.question: If you are on Notion’s Free Plan, which of the following is true about automations in a duplicated template?

options.text: You can edit existing automations but cannot create new ones
options.explanation: The article says you cannot edit them on the Free Plan.

options.text: You can use and turn off automations, but cannot edit or create new ones
options.explanation: Free Plan users can toggle automations but not edit or add new ones.

options.text: Automations are completely disabled and cannot be turned on
options.explanation: You can still use and turn them off, just not edit.

options.text: All automations are automatically upgraded to premium features
options.explanation: Free Plan does not grant premium automation capabilities.

questions.question: Under what condition will Notion decline a refund request for a Marketplace purchase?

options.text: If the request is made after 30 days from purchase
options.explanation: The policy allows refunds within 14 days, not 30.

options.text: If the user has made multiple refund requests in a short period, indicating abusive behavior
options.explanation: Notion may decline refunds for abusive patterns, as stated.

options.text: If the template was purchased on a third‑party platform
options.explanation: Refunds for third‑party purchases are not handled by Notion, but the question asks about a Marketplace refund request.

options.text: If the user no longer wants the template but has used it for more than 5 days
options.explanation: Usage time isn’t a listed reason for denial; only abusive behavior is mentioned.

questions.question: What happens to copies of a template in your workspace after a refund is approved?

options.text: All copies, including any edits and added pages, are removed
options.explanation: The policy states that all copies and edits are removed after a refund.

options.text: Only the original duplicated page is removed; any derived pages remain
options.explanation: The article says all copies, even derived pages, are removed.

options.text: The template is archived but remains viewable in read‑only mode
options.explanation: Refund removal is complete, not archival.

options.text: The template is replaced with a blank page placeholder
options.explanation: The content is fully deleted, not replaced with a placeholder.
